name: Prashar Lake Trek
aboutText: Prashar Lake is famous for its mirror-like waters and the three-storey pagoda temple. The trail is peaceful and photogenic.

itineraryJson.title: Day 1: Reach Mandi and drive to trailhead; camp nearby
itineraryJson.highlights: Arrival in Mandi, drive to trailhead and set up camp nearby.

itineraryJson.title: Day 2: Trek to Prashar Lake, visit island temple and camp
itineraryJson.highlights: Trek to Prashar Lake, visit the island temple and set up camp.

itineraryJson.title: Day 3: Return to Mandi and depart
itineraryJson.highlights: Return to Mandi, concluding the trek.

difficultyDetails.justification: The Prashar Lake Trek is considered easy due to its short duration and relatively flat terrain. The trek involves a gradual incline, making it accessible to trekkers of all ages and fitness levels.
difficultyDetails.fitnessRequirement: Must be able to walk 6-8 hours daily on uneven terrain and handle moderate altitude changes.
difficultyDetails.priorExperience: No prior trekking experience is required, but previous experience with similar terrain would be beneficial.

January.weather: Cold, frosty mornings with occasional snowfall

February.weather: Crisp and cold, with occasional light snowfall

March.weather: Mild temperatures, with some snowfall in the higher altitudes

April.weather: Temperatures rise, with occasional rain showers

May.weather: Warm and sunny, with some occasional rain showers

June.weather: Hot and dry, with low humidity

July.weather: Extremely hot and dry, with high risk of heat stroke

August.weather: Hot and humid, with occasional rain showers

September.weather: Warm and sunny, with some occasional rain showers

October.weather: Mild temperatures, with some snowfall in the higher altitudes

November.weather: Cold and frosty, with occasional light snowfall

December.weather: Very cold and snowy, with high risk of avalanches

bestTimeJson.peakSeasonPros: The best time to visit Prashar Lake Trek is during the peak season (May-Oct) when the weather is generally clear and sunny, making it ideal for trekking. The temperatures are mild, and the trail conditions are mostly open.
bestTimeJson.peakSeasonCons: During peak season, the trek can get crowded, and prices may be higher due to increased demand. Additionally, the heat in July-August can be extreme, posing a risk to trekkers.
bestTimeJson.offSeasonPros: Trekking during the off-season (November-May) has its benefits, such as lower prices, fewer crowds, and a more serene experience. The scenery is also picturesque, with snow-covered landscapes and frozen lakes.
bestTimeJson.offSeasonCons: The off-season can be challenging due to harsh weather conditions, including cold temperatures, snowfall, and high risk of avalanches. Some trails may be closed or difficult to navigate.

gettingThere.nearestAirport: Gaggal Airport (Shimla), approximately 120 km from Mandi
gettingThere.nearestRailway: Jogindernagar Railway Station, approximately 15 km from Mandi
gettingThere.fromDelhi: Drive to Shimla ( approx. 7-8 hours) and then take a taxi or bus to Mandi
gettingThere.fromMumbai: Fly to Chandigarh Airport (approx. 1.5 hours), then drive to Mandi (approx. 2-3 hours)
gettingThere.localTransport: Regular buses and taxis are available from Mandi to the trek base, Prashar Lake
gettingThere.summary: Reaching Mandi is relatively easy, with Gaggal Airport nearby and regular bus services. From there, take a taxi or bus to Prashar Village, the starting point of your trek.

permits.innerLinePermitDetails: Required for trekking in border areas
permits.restrictions: No restrictions on photography or camping
permits.summary: No permits required for standard treks, but Inner Line Permit is needed for border areas.

seo.title: Prashar Lake Trek – Easy Himalayan Adventure | Trail Makers
seo.description: Explore Prashar Lake in Himachal Pradesh, a 3-day easy trek with breathtaking views.
